title: Pungent Crisp Malaysian Ackee Satay
description: A rich and savory Malaysian-inspired dish featuring ackee, perfect for a date night dinner.
instructions: Soak the bamboo skewers in water for at least 30 minutes.
Cut the ackee into bite-sized pieces.
Cut the lemongrass and galangal into thin slices.
Thread the ackee, lemongrass, and galangal onto the bamboo skewers.
Heat 2 tablespoons of peanut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the kaffir lime leaves and cook for 1 minute.
Add the skewers and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Transfer the skewers to a plate and set aside.
Add the remaining 2 tablespoons of peanut oil to the skillet.
Add the palm sugar, fish sauce, lime juice, salt, and black pepper to the skillet.
Cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the sauce has thickened and reduced.
Add the coconut milk to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Return the skewers to the skillet and coat with the sauce.
Serve immediately and garnish with additional kaffir lime leaves if desired.
